greeting.text: Mind the webs, love. I've made this hollow my workshop — the burrow above drips with the finest silk in the wood, and no smith's iron will ever sit as light on a caster's shoulders as my cloth. Bring me thread and I'll sew you something worth wearing.

sew.text: Spider silk makes gloves and slippers light as a held breath — clarity for a wandering mind. Weave it with grave linen and I'll cut you a robe or leggings a mage would kill for. And when your hands are sure enough, I'll sew you a Shroudweave Hood fit for an archmage. Type SEW here and I'll show you my patterns.

cloth.text: The giant spiders in the burrow just north trail silk from every rafter — cut them down and take it. For grave linen you'll go deeper, into the Sunless Catacombs beneath the wood; the burial dead are wrapped in it still. GATHER what the crypts give up and bring it back to me.

end.text: Off you go, then. And do try not to bleed on the merchandise.
